name: Liu Bei 劉備

sections.heading: Style Name
sections.content: Xuande(P: Xuándé WG: Hsüan-tê)(TC: 玄德 SC: 玄德 (same))

sections.heading: Home
sections.content: Zhuo county in Zhuo commandery, You province

sections.heading: Died
sections.content:
May 223

SGZ: Shu 2

Zhāolièdì 昭烈帝

Hàn Zhāoliè huángdì 漢昭烈皇帝

• Han• Gongsun Zan• Tao Qian• Cao Cao• Yuan Shao• Liu Bei• Shu-Han

• Yellow Turban Rebellion• Battle of Xiapi• Battle of Bowang Slope• Battle of Chibi

Liu Bei was born in Zhuo commandery in 161 A.D. His family claimed descent from Emperor Jing of Former Han through the kings of Zhongshan and a short-lived fief in Zhuo commandery held by a cadet lineage.

His grandfather, Liu Xiong 劉雄, was a country magistrate and his father Liu Hong 劉弘 held office in the local administration.

Liu Bei lost his father when he was still young and Liu Bei lived a poor life. His mother maintained the family by selling straw sandals. In a corner of their small property there was a mulberry tree over fifteen feet tall,[1] shaped like an umbrella of the chariot. The tree held some superstitious value, people who saw it from afar took it as a sign of future fame and honour. Liu Bei agreed on this, but his uncle Liu Zijing 劉子敬 suggested that Liu Bei would only bring his family to ruin.[2]

In 175 A.D. Liu Bei was sent to study by his mother. He took lessons from Lu Zhi, a distinguished man of the commandery who was an Academician at the University of Luoyang.

sections.content:
Liu Bei was born in Zhuo commandery in 161 A.D. His family claimed descent from Emperor Jing of Former Han through the kings of Zhongshan and a short-lived fief in Zhuo commandery held by a cadet lineage.

Liu Bei lost his father when he was still young and Liu Bei lived a poor life. His mother maintained the family by selling straw sandals. In a corner of their small property there was a mulberry tree over fifteen feet tall,[1] shaped like an umbrella of the chariot. The tree held some superstitious value, people who saw it from afar took it as a sign of future fame and honour. Liu Bei agreed on this, but his uncle Liu Zijing 劉子敬 suggested that Liu Bei would only bring his family to ruin.[2]

sections.heading: Fact vs. Fiction
sections.content:
- Historically, Liu Bei did not swear an oath of brotherhood with Zhang Fei and Guan Yu.read about this fictional event here.

- Historically, Liu Bei, Zhang Fei and Guan Yu did not rescue Dong Zhuo during the Yellow Turban Rebellion.

- ↑ Rafe de Crespigny, A Biographical Dictionary of the Later Han, page 478.
